qa(005): confirm all zero-friction acceptance criteria pass (#5)

Audit of index.html against all six acceptance criteria:
- No login/auth screen: app renders task input directly on load
- No personal data collection: only task text input, no email/name/password
- Task input is first interactive element: #new-task-input is first in DOM order
- Sub-10s time-to-first-task: single self-contained file, zero external requests
- No tracking/analytics: no third-party scripts, pixels, or CDN resources
- No promotional prompts: no account creation flows or notification requests

All six acceptance criteria pass. Implementation is correct and complete.

fix: prevent duplicate CowSwap orders on retry + execution guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Complete Phase 3 - Bayesian Synthesis, Strategy Layer, Backtesting, Integration Tests

DELIVERABLES:

1. Bayesian Synthesis Layer (synthesis/mod.rs)
   - BayesianCalibrator: Platt scaling for posterior calibration
     * f(x) = 1/(1+exp(A*x+B)), default A=-2.0, B=1.0
     * naive_bayes_fusion(): log-odds combination of independent pillars
   - RegimePriorTable: Regime-specific base rates
     * Bull=58%, Bear=55%, Choppy=48%, Volatile=52%, Calm=53%
     * Online Bayesian update via update_prior()
   - KellySizer: Half-Kelly risk sizing with max cap
     * f* = (b*p - q) / b * kelly_multiplier
     * Capped at max_kelly_fraction (default 25%)
   - SignalFuser: Three-pillar combination
     * Correlation discount (0.70) prevents overconfidence
     * Profit factor estimated by regime
   - Synthesizer: Main entry point
     * Bayesian path: posterior + EV gating
     * Simple threshold fallback
     * Batch synthesis with deduplication
     * Signals sorted by quality_score()
   - Tests: 12 unit tests (100% pass)

2. Strategy Integration Layer (strategies/mod.rs)
   - Strategy trait: strategy_type() + evaluate()
   - PHOENIX: Momentum resurrection
     * Triggers on: pre_emergence>0.65, sub_linear<0.40, Bear/Choppy regime
   - PHANTOM: Liquidity void hunter
     * Triggers on: sub_linear>0.70 (anomalous order book)
     * Sell when sub_linear>0.85 (panic), else Buy
   - ECHO: Cross-asset resonance
     * Triggers on: negative_space>0.65, confidence>0.60
   - ORACLE: Pre-earnings positioning
     * Triggers on: pre_emergence>0.70, confidence>0.65
   - SENTINEL: Regime transition alpha
     * Triggers on: combined>0.72, multi-pillar elevation
   - HYDRA: Multi-timeframe consensus
     * Triggers when ALL three pillars are elevated (>0.50)
   - StrategyRouter: Routes signals to all 6 strategies
     * route() -> Vec<StrategySignal> (all matching strategies)
     * route_top(n) -> top-n by confidence
   - Tests: 12 strategy + router unit tests

3. Backtesting Framework (backtesting/mod.rs)
   - MarketScenario: 4 historical scenarios
     * Bull2021: trending bull market
     * GFC2008: 2008 financial crisis crash
     * Covid2020: COVID crash + recovery
     * RateHike2022: Fed rate hike bear market
   - Synthetic OHLCV event generation per scenario
   - BacktestMetrics: Full performance analytics
     * win_rate, profit_factor, max_drawdown
     * Sharpe ratio (annualised), Sortino ratio
     * total_return, signals_accepted rate
   - BacktestEngine: Event-driven backtest runner
     * Routes events through StrategyRouter
     * Simulates position entry/exit with slippage=0.001
   - BacktestReport: Human-readable summary
   - Tests: 6 backtesting unit tests

4. Integration Tests (core/tests/integration_test.rs)
   - test_full_pipeline_btc: BTC/USD end-to-end
   - test_full_pipeline_eth: ETH/USD end-to-end
   - test_bayesian_calibration_consistency: Monotonicity check
   - test_all_six_strategies_initialise: All 6 strategies load
   - test_strategy_router_routes_signals: Router routes correctly
   - test_backtest_bull_scenario: Bull2021 metrics check
   - test_backtest_crash_scenario: GFC2008 metrics check
   - test_backtest_covid_scenario: Covid2020 metrics check
   - test_paper_trading_simulation: 100 events, no errors
   - test_batch_synthesize_multi_symbol: Multi-symbol dedup
   - Total: 10 integration tests

BUILD STATUS:
  Compilation: 0 errors
  lib tests: 108 passed (0 failed)
  integration tests: 10 passed (0 failed)
  Total: 118 tests passing (100% success rate)

Also: Add tmpclaude-* to .gitignore to exclude agent temp files.

commit.message:
Build schema-driven frontmatter panel component (#7)

Replaces the generic key-value FrontmatterEditor with a new FrontmatterPanel that renders proper field types (text, textarea, date, tags, toggle, select) based on collection schemas. The panel sits in a collapsible right sidebar in the editor layout. A new useCollectionSchema hook fetches collections and matches file paths to schemas. Falls back to generic editor when no schema matches.

commit.message:
feat: initial release of Oil and Gas Engineering Claude Skills library

7 domain-specific skills for petroleum engineering workflows:
- Oil & Gas Data Manager (flagship multi-domain file detection + extraction)
- Drilling KPI Analyzer (DDR parsing, ROP, NPT, MSE, benchmarking)
- Well Log Interpreter (LAS/DLIS parsing, pay zone identification, Vsh/Sw)
- Completion Design Reviewer (frac stage validation, proppant/fluid analysis)
- Production Data Analyst (Arps decline curves, IP rates, EUR estimation)
- Directional Survey Analyzer (minimum curvature, DLS, anticollision)
- HSE Incident Tracker (safety event extraction, IADC classification, TRIR)

Full Python implementation for Oil & Gas Data Manager including:
- LAS, DLIS, PDF, XLSX, CSV, DOCX parsers
- Pydantic extraction schemas
- Project matching with confidence scoring
- Engineering sanity checks
- pytest test suite

commit.message:
Make platforms ultra-visible and each difficulty a visually distinct game

Platform visibility:
- 8px outer glow halos on all surfaces (platforms, walls, moving, falling)
- 6px thick bright top edges with 5px glow above
- 4px side borders on everything
- Boost pads get outer glow and bright border
- Spikes get stronger pulsing glow

Difficulty differentiation - each is a different game:
- EASY: Calm blue theme, wider platforms (+3 tiles), fewer spikes (50% removed),
  slower moving platforms, longer falling platform delay, weaker gravity
- MEDIUM: Neon purple theme (original), standard layout and physics
- HARD: Fiery orange/red theme, narrower platforms (-1 tile), wider gaps,
  faster moving platforms (1.3x), shorter falling delay, stronger gravity
- EXTREME: Blood red/black theme, tiny platforms (-2 tiles), much wider gaps,
  very fast platforms (1.6x), near-instant falling, pixel-perfect physics

Each difficulty has unique colors for: background, stars, platforms, walls,
moving platforms, falling platforms, player character, and all glow effects.

fix: add ngrok-skip-browser-warning header to auth.ts fetch calls

fetchUserProfile and signUp were missing the header, causing ngrok

Stream yt-dlp output for long video downloads

Replace subprocess.run (which buffers all output) with Popen streaming.
This provides real-time download progress to the UI, prevents memory
buildup from thousands of progress lines on long videos, and allows
cancellation to interrupt the download immediately.

commit.message:
feat: node/client cert creation, TLS 1.3 integration, and CA verification

Major additions to the pqx library:

x509pq:
- CreateNodeCert: node certificates with SAN extensions (DNS + IP), signed by CA
- CreateClientCert: client certificates with ExtKeyUsageClientAuth, signed by CA
- VerifyCertWithCA: verify leaf cert PQC signatures against CA keys
- Extension mode: sign SPKI (stable across extension changes) instead of TBS,
  store CA's ML-DSA key in extension for proper chain verification
- SAN/EKU extension builders for pure/composite modes (bypass Go's x509)

tlspq:
- ServerConfig: TLS 1.3 server with pqx cert, optional mutual TLS
- ClientConfig: TLS 1.3 client with CA trust and PQC verifier callback
- MutualTLSConfig: matched server/client configs
- VerifyPeerCertificate callback for PQC signature validation
- Graceful degradation: non-pqx certs pass classical verification

30 tests passing including a live TLS 1.3 handshake with mutual auth,
PQC certificate verification, and data exchange.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix skeleton disconnected lines: trace junction-to-junction chains

The root bug: interior chains between two junctions (the majority of
connected line art) were never traced. _traceBranch only starts from
endpoint pixels (n=1), so junction→interior→junction segments were
entirely skipped — producing isolated dashes instead of connected lines.

Fix: add Pass 2 that iterates every junction and, for each unvisited
interior neighbour, calls _traceBranchFromJunction to walk the chain
through interior pixels until hitting another junction. Both junction
endpoints are included in the branch so paths visually connect.
Also handles direct junction-junction adjacency via an edge-dedup set.

Co-authored-by: Varun Vaid <varunvaid@Varuns-MacBook-Air.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: harden HTTP security (body size limit, error sanitization, health endpoint) (#113)

* fix: harden HTTP security (body size limit, error sanitization, health endpoint)

- Add 1 MiB request body size limit via maxBytesMiddleware (#48)
- Return 413 with descriptive error when limit exceeded
- Sanitize writeServiceErrorResponse fallback to return generic
  "Internal server error" instead of raw err.Error() (#49)
- Remove storage error details from health endpoint response (#50)
- Update OpenAPI spec with 413 responses and PayloadTooLarge schema
- Update SECURITY.md and api.md documentation
- Add tests for all three security fixes

Closes #48, closes #49, closes #50

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove ContentLength guard bypass in maxBytesMiddleware

The ContentLength != 0 check allowed clients to bypass the body size
limit by omitting or zeroing the Content-Length header while still
streaming a large body. http.MaxBytesReader handles zero-length bodies
correctly, so the guard was unnecessary and created a bypass vector.

commit.message:
perf(hashlife): reduce lock acquisitions in step_universe (#14)

* perf(hashlife): reduce lock acquisitions in step_universe

Closes #6

src/hashlife.rs:
- Add needs_expansion_inner and needs_expansion_deep_inner as private
  free functions taking &[Node] instead of acquiring a lock, eliminating
  redundant mutex overhead on each call
- Rewrite step_universe expansion loop from while+two-method-calls (2
  lock acquisitions per iteration) to a single loop { lock once; check
  all three conditions; break or expand_root }
- Merge two back-to-back result-node lock acquisitions after
  step_recursive into a single lock scope
- Delete the old needs_expansion and needs_expansion_deep &self methods
  which are now superseded by the free functions
- Update tests test_needs_expansion_catches_edge_not_corner and
  test_needs_expansion_deep_catches_near_boundary to call the free
  functions directly with an inline lock

Benchmark results (before vs after):
- pulsar: -8.6% (improved)
- gosper_gun: +1% (within noise)
- cordership_gun: no change (p=0.57)
- large_soup: no change (p=0.75)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(claude): update CLAUDE.md invariant names to match refactored free functions

`needs_expansion()` and `needs_expansion_deep()` were converted to
`needs_expansion_inner()` and `needs_expansion_deep_inner()` free
functions in the PR; update the two references in the key invariants
and HashLife architecture sections of CLAUDE.md accordingly.

- CLAUDE.md: replace stale `needs_expansion()` / `needs_expansion_deep()`
  method-name references with `needs_expansion_inner()` /
  `needs_expansion_deep_inner()`, and note the single-lock-per-iteration
  design in the expansion loop description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix accuracy: recent-regime Hurst, recency-weighted behavioral, fresh cache

- Reduce FMP historical data cache from 4hr to 5min for fresh daily data
- Increase behavioral lookback from 20 to 50 bars with recency weighting
  (recent bars count 2x more than older bars)
- Add recent-regime Hurst (60-bar window) to mood classifier, blended
  60/40 with full-series Hurst so recent moves aren't masked
- Detect regime shifts when recent H diverges >0.08 from full-series H
- Regime breakdown suppresses Euphoria (-20) and boosts Panic (+20)
- Lower fear thresholds for earlier detection of selling pressure
- Display regime-change warning in MoodIndicator component
- Update backtest engine to pass daily closes to mood classifier
